_comment: FIDELITY-KERNEL demonstrator. The BRIEF asks for a feature-map encoding circuit (the same feature-bound-op mechanism as qml_sign1) whose judged quantity is the state-overlap kernel |<phi(x)|phi(y)>|^2 between two classical data points, estimated WITHOUT an ancilla/SWAP-test register: the judge instantiates the model's own template independently for x and for y and takes the overlap of the two returned statevectors directly (sim.fidelity). The VISIBLE pair is a near-pair (x, y almost identical) the encoding must call SIMILAR (kernel >= kernel_min); the HELD-OUT pair is a far-pair (x, y very different) it must call DISSIMILAR (kernel <= holdout.kernel_max). A degenerate feature map that ignores its input (e.g. scale 0 -> every point maps to |00>) trivially nails the visible pair (constant overlap 1.0) but fails the held-out pair (still 1.0, not <= kernel_max) -- REJECTED at the anti-overfit gate (exit 6), the same trap qml_sign1 sets with a high-frequency encoding.
